rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARGUERITA L. RUBEL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner D. R. Rubel'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Attorney'S
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's motion to disqualify John A. Hartog, individually from representing Donald Crabtree, Respondent and Trustee, is granted. John A. Hartog has agreed to recusal and has further agreed to be bound by an order not to discuss, divulge or disclose anything related to this case, and not to have any involvement in the representation of Donald Crabtree. That shall also be the order of the court. The motion to disqualify attorneys other than John A. Hartog at Hartog & Associates, Inc., including David W. Baer is denied. Shadow Traffic sets forth a three part test to be applied: (1) did Connor impart confidential information communicated to Hartog; (2) was it shared with counsel for respondent; and (3) is disqualification of the entire Hartog firm and/or the entire Goldstein firm warranted. Part 1 is met, parts 2 and 3 are not. First, there is sufficient showing that Connors held a pre-retention of expert telephone conference with John A. Hartog in which confidential information was disclosed to Hartog and that Connors had a reasonable expectation of confidentiality. Based upon the declarations, there is a rebuttable presumption that confidential information was disclosed. Second, Baer rebutted the presumption that Hartog disclosed any confidential information to him, or any other member of the Hartog or Goldstein firm, or their staff. Third, based upon the facts before the court disqualification of the entire Hartog firm is unwarranted. Petitioner's request to disqualify the firm of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran, LLP is also denied. There is no showing that any confidential information was imparted by either Connor or Hartog to the Goldstein firm. Any party who wishes to contest the tentative ruling and argue the matter must notify opposing counsel and the court (415-551-3702) by 5:00 p.m. on October 27, 2011.
